title: TourBuddy Front Dashboard Interface

requirements.text: Create a top navigation bar with the TourBuddy logo/brand name, a global alert status indicator (e.g., 'All Clear', 'Active Incidents'), and a user profile/logout area.

quotes: just create the front dashboard interface

acceptanceCriteria: Navigation bar is visible at the top of every dashboard page
Brand name 'TourBuddy' is displayed prominently
Global alert status badge is visible with appropriate color coding
User profile area is present in the top-right

requirements.text: Create a left sidebar with navigation links for: Overview (home), Incidents, Geo-fence Alerts, Risk Heatmap, and Volunteer Management. Highlight the active section.

quotes: just create the front dashboard interface

acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar contains all five navigation items
Active navigation item is visually highlighted
Sidebar is collapsible or fixed on desktop layout

requirements.text: Create a main Overview page as the dashboard home. Display four KPI summary cards: Total Active Incidents, Active Geo-fence Alerts, Volunteers On Duty, and Tourist Areas Monitored. Each card shows a count and a trend indicator.

quotes: just create the front dashboard interface

acceptanceCriteria: Four KPI cards are displayed in a responsive grid
Each card shows a label, a numeric value (mocked/static), and a trend arrow
Cards adapt layout for different screen sizes

requirements.text: Create a Recent Incidents table section on the Overview page showing columns: Incident ID, Location, Type, Severity (color-coded: Low/Medium/High/Critical), Status, and Reported Time. Populate with realistic mock data.

quotes: just create the front dashboard interface

acceptanceCriteria: Table displays at least 6 mock incident rows
Severity is shown as a color-coded badge
Status column shows Open/In Progress/Resolved states
Table is scrollable on smaller screens

requirements.text: Create a Risk Heatmap placeholder section that displays a stylized SVG-based zone map with color-coded risk zones (green = safe, yellow = caution, red = high risk) representing tourist areas. Use static mock data for zone risk levels.

quotes: just create the front dashboard interface

acceptanceCriteria: Heatmap section renders with at least 6 labeled zones
Zones are colored according to risk level (green/yellow/red)
A legend explaining the color coding is visible

requirements.text: Create an Active Geo-fence Alerts panel on the Overview page listing current alerts with zone name, alert type, triggered time, and a dismiss/acknowledge button (UI only, no backend).

quotes: just create the front dashboard interface

acceptanceCriteria: Panel lists at least 3 mock geo-fence alerts
Each alert shows zone name, type, and triggered time
Acknowledge button is clickable and removes the alert from the list client-side

requirements.text: Create a Volunteer Management section accessible from the sidebar, showing a list/table of volunteers with: Name, Badge ID, Area Assigned, Status (Active/Off Duty/On Break), and Last Check-in time. Populate with mock data.

quotes: just create the front dashboard interface

acceptanceCriteria: Volunteer list displays at least 8 mock entries
Status is shown with color-coded badge
Table is filterable by status (Active/Off Duty) using a dropdown or tab UI

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive visual theme across the entire dashboard: dark navy/slate background with amber/gold accents for alerts and highlights, clean sans-serif typography, card-based layout with subtle shadows and borders. The aesthetic should evoke authority and situational awareness appropriate for a safety command center.

quotes: just create the front dashboard interface

acceptanceCriteria: Consistent color palette (dark navy background, amber/gold accents) is used throughout
All pages share the same typography and spacing system
Cards and panels have consistent border-radius, shadow, and padding

requirements.text: Render the generated dashboard hero/logo image as a static asset from frontend/public/assets/generated.
acceptanceCriteria: Image is referenced from frontend/public/assets/generated
Image is displayed in the sidebar or navigation branding area

constraints: All data displayed on the dashboard must use static mock data — no backend integration required for this build
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui
Do not implement authentication flows in this build
nonGoals: Actual geolocation or map API integration

required: TourBuddy shield-and-compass logo mark with a dark navy and amber color scheme, flat vector style (tourbuddy-logo.dim_256x256.png)
